title: laurus regia
intro: Laurus regia, commonly known as bay laurel, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region that belongs to the laurel family. The medicinal properties of Laurus regia include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and compounds like eugenol and caryophyllene. The health benefits of bay laurel include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu due to its expectorant properties. Bay laurel is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, which can be used to add flavor and medicinal value to food, beverages, and herbal remedies.
